definitions: A sleep disorder characterized by repeated episodes of upper airway obstruction during sleep, leading to pauses in breathing or shallow breathing.
A condition where the throat muscles relax and block the airway during sleep, causing breathing to repeatedly stop and start.
examples: He was diagnosed with obstructive sleep apnoea.
Treatment for obstructive sleep apnoea can improve quality of life.
Snoring is a common symptom of obstructive sleep apnoea.
